explanation:
The input requirement says that, when either PCV or PSV mode is selected and the GUI sets Inspiratory Pause, there must be the possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause. We treat 'possibility' as the system enabling this to occur whenever these conditions are true. Formally: whenever (PCVModeSelected OR PSVModeSelected) AND inspiratoryPauseButton, it must be possible (eventually) to reach inspiratoryPause. This is captured by a response property (globally, always when the mode is PCV or PSV and the button is set, then eventually inspiratoryPause occurs within a certain duration).

Therefore, we use the template:
G (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)), where:
bool_exp2 = (PCVModeSelected | PSVModeSelected) & inspiratoryPauseButton
bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPause
N_DURATION = 10 (an example reasonable fixed value)

This ensures that whenever in PCV or PSV mode with the button set, an inspiratoryPause will eventually (within N_DURATION steps) occur (be possible).

The property expresses that whenever the ventilator is in either Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCVMode) or Pressure Support Ventilation (PSVMode) mode, and the Inspiratory Pause button is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), the system should eventually initiate an Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause). This is modeled by the LTL template: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> F[0,5] inspiratoryPause). The boolean expression for conjunction of being in the correct mode and the button being set is: (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton. inspiratoryPause is the expected effect, and the time bound 5 is chosen as a reasonable system delay to respond. This captures the requirement that in these modes, if the GUI requests it, the Inspiratory Pause must be possible.

The requirement is: In PCV and PSV modes there shall be the possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause if it is set by the GUI. This means that whenever the ventilator is in either PCVMode or PSVMode, and the user (via the GUI) sets the Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPauseButton), then there must be a possibility to initiate the Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause becomes true at some point). To capture this, we use template 14, which expresses: whenever (in PCVMode or PSVMode) and inspiratoryPauseButton is true, then within a bounded time N_DURATION (here set as 3), inspiratoryPause must become true. This ensures that if the user requests an Inspiratory Pause in the relevant modes, it will be possible to initiate it within a reasonable time.

The requirement states that in PCV and PSV modes, it should be possible to initiate an Inspiratory Pause if that pause is set (requested) by the GUI. This means: Whenever the ventilator is operating in either PCV or PSV mode and the inspiratoryPauseButton is set (by the user via the GUI), then the system should allow (i.e., guarantee the possibility of) inspiratoryPause to be active. This is best captured by an invariant ("globally, whenever the precondition holds, the response must hold"). The precondition (bool_exp2) is: either PCVMode OR PSVMode AND inspiratoryPauseButton (the mode is either PCV or PSV, and the GUI has requested the pause). The response (bool_exp3) is inspiratoryPause (the pause is indeed active/possible). There are no additional subconditions (bool_exp1, bool_exp4). N_DURATION is not needed. The template G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3) expresses: Globally, if (PCV or PSV) & inspiratoryPauseButton, then inspiratoryPause is possible/active.

The property states that, always (globally), if the system is in either PCV mode or PSV mode AND the GUI has set the Inspiratory Pause button (inspiratoryPauseButton), then it shall be possible to initiate an inspiratory pause (i.e., inspiratoryPause must become true). This directly matches the requirement that in both PCV and PSV, when the GUI has called for an inspiratory pause, the pause must be allowed. This is best captured by an implication, globally, from (mode is PCV or PSV AND pause is required) to (pause is possible).

The natural language states that when in PCV or PSV ventilation modes, the system must allow the possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause IF the Inspiratory Pause is set via the GUI. This is best captured by stating: whenever the system is in either PCV or PSV mode (PCVMode OR PSVMode) and the inspiratoryPauseButton is set (by GUI), then an inspiratoryPause can occur. The chosen template expresses this as: globally, if (PCVMode OR PSVMode) AND inspiratoryPauseButton, then it is possible to have inspiratoryPause. (We only relate the two events according to the description, not enforcing that inspiratoryPause will always immediately occur, but that if required, it is permitted.)

The requirement is that if the mode is set to PCV or PSV, and the inspiratory pause is requested from the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then there shall be a possibility (i.e., potential/allowed state) to initiate an Inspiratory Pause. In this translation, we take 'there shall be the possibility' to imply that whenever (PCVMode or PSVMode) is active and inspiratoryPauseButton is set (i.e., pressed), then inspiratoryPause must eventually occur. This is captured using a liveness property: whenever (PCVMode or PSVMode) and inspiratoryPauseButton are true, then eventually inspiratoryPause will occur within a specified bounded time (using F[0,N]). This fits in the template: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (F[0,N] bool_exp3)), where bool_exp1 is PCVMode, bool_exp2 is PSVMode | PCVMode (we conjoin with the pause button press), and bool_exp3 is inspiratoryPause (the action to take).

Here, bool_exp1 = (PCVMode | PSVMode) ensures either mode is active; bool_exp2 = inspiratoryPauseButton detects the GUI-set pause; bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPause is the intended result. N_DURATION is set to 5 (an arbitrary small finite bound).

The natural language requirement specifies that when the ventilator is operating in either PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) or PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) modes and the GUI (Graphical User Interface) requests an Inspiratory Pause (by setting the respective button), it must be possible for the system to initiate the Inspiratory Pause function. This corresponds to stating: Whenever the ventilator is in PCVMode or PSVMode and 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is set by the GUI, then (eventually) 'inspiratoryPause' should become true (i.e., the Inspiratory Pause is active). Thus, for every time PCVMode or PSVMode and inspiratoryPauseButton are active, the 'inspiratoryPause' must occur within a bounded time. This is best captured by a bounded response (template 14: G (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))). Here, bool_exp2 is (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton (the precondition), and bool_exp3 is inspiratoryPause (the consequence). N_DURATION is set to 3 as a reasonable default, but can be adjusted based on system requirements. This ensures that in the specified modes, if the GUI requests an Inspiratory Pause, it is possible and will happen within a small, bounded time.

The requirement is that in PCV or PSV modes, if the inspiratory pause is set via the GUI (i.e., the user has initiated it), then there should be the possibility for an inspiratory pause to occur. To capture this, we model it so that whenever the mode is PCV or PSV, and the inspiratory pause button is pressed, the system must eventually enter an inspiratoryPause state. This captures the causal requirement that when the preconditions are met, inspiratoryPause should be possible. We use "(PCVMode | PSVMode)" to represent either operating mode, and "inspiratoryPauseButton" for the GUI request. We combine the two as the precondition, and require "inspiratoryPause" to eventually follow under these circumstances. This is modeled by the template: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> F[0,N] bool_exp3) (template 44).

The requirement states that in both PCV and PSV modes, it must be possible to initiate an inspiratory pause if (and only if) it is set by the GUI. Translating, this means: 'if either Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCVMode) or Pressure Support Ventilation Mode (PSVMode) is active, and the GUI button for inspiratory pause (inspiratoryPauseButton) is activated, then an inspiratory pause (inspiratoryPause) should occur.' This is best captured by a global LTL formula expressing that whenever the mode is PCV or PSV and the GUI button is pressed, then the inspiratory pause must occur (possibly immediately or within a short time, but the language does not specify a delay, so direct implication is used). The template encoding this is: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), where bool_exp1 is (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton and bool_exp3 is inspiratoryPause.
